name: Temple Scribe
shortDescription: Sacred record-keeper trained in doctrine, texts, and ritual learning.
contextNotes: Preserves doctrine and manages sacred records.

name: Court Scribe / Clerk
shortDescription: Office-trained writer and record-keeper serving a ruler, court, or bureaucracy.
contextNotes: Handles petitions, decrees, correspondence, and official records.

name: Local Trader
shortDescription: Market trader dealing in ordinary goods, prices, and practical exchange.
contextNotes: Most common merchant subtype.

name: Household Courtier
shortDescription: Court insider skilled in favor, ceremony, and household politics.
contextNotes: Thrives in noble households and princely courts.

name: Folk Healer
shortDescription: Practical local healer using herbs, urgent care, and traditional knowledge.
contextNotes: Belongs to village, clan, and low-literacy settings.

name: Builder / Master Mason
shortDescription: Construction specialist focused on durable works and physical building.
contextNotes: Emphasizes stone, timber, layout, and practical site work.

name: Deck Sailor
shortDescription: Working crewman focused on routine shipboard labor and seamanship.
contextNotes: Baseline sailor.

name: Herdsman
shortDescription: Rural stock-worker managing grazing animals and everyday animal care.
contextNotes: Clearest pastoral subtype.

name: Burglar
shortDescription: Technical intruder focused on entry, locks, traps, and hidden access.
contextNotes: Classic controlled-entry thief.

name: Folk Performer
shortDescription: Public entertainer using broad expressive skills for common audiences.
contextNotes: Oldest and widest form of the profession.

name: Tribal Warrior
shortDescription: Clan or tribal fighter shaped by raid, hunt, and direct violence.
contextNotes: Best for low-organization war cultures where fieldcraft and personal combat matter more than drill.

name: Levy Infantry
shortDescription: Common soldier with basic organized infantry training.
contextNotes: Basic but coherent troop package.

name: Cavalry / Mounted Retainer
shortDescription: Mounted combatant using mobility and multiple mounted weapons in war.
contextNotes: Proper cavalry should normally know both Lance and 1-handed Edged plus Ride, Dodge, and Parry.

name: Actor
shortDescription: Role-based performer using speech, presentation, and imitation for stage or social effect.
contextNotes: This subtype fits staged performance, civic spectacle, court entertainment, and social impersonation traditions.

name: Animal Trainer
shortDescription: Working-animal specialist trained to condition beasts for labor, transport, hunting, or war.
contextNotes: Best for roles where animals must be taught controlled tasks beyond basic care.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Assassin
shortDescription: Covert killer trained in stealth, approach, and controlled lethal action.
contextNotes: Belongs in criminal, political, military, or clandestine settings where murder is deliberate and specialized rather than merely violent.

name: Bandit
shortDescription: Predatory outlaw using ambush, intimidation, and opportunistic violence.
contextNotes: Best for rural roads, frontier spaces, and weakly policed routes where direct theft and force matter more than urban finesse.

name: Beggar
shortDescription: Street survivalist trained in reading people, soliciting support, and moving under notice without consequence.
contextNotes: Urban margin subtype that may overlap with informants, thieves, or social survivors.

name: Bodyguard
shortDescription: Protective combat specialist focused on keeping a client or patron alive in dangerous situations.
contextNotes: Protective martial specialist serving an individual, household, or patron.

name: Bounty Hunter
shortDescription: Pursuit and capture specialist using tracking-adjacent search, intimidation, and controlled violence.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation. Reassigned from Herdsman/Rider to Thief/Infiltrator because the role is primarily pursuit, coercive search, urban/frontier tracking, and controlled violence rather than animal-working.
notes: Completed package in review pass. Family corrected to Thief/Infiltrator.

name: Bureaucrat
shortDescription: Office-trained administrator handling records, routine governance, and institutional procedure.
contextNotes: This subtype fits literate state, temple, civic, and court systems where regular office work, records, taxation, and controlled procedure matter more than broad scholarly learning.
notes: Completed package in review pass. Office/civil cluster.

name: Caravan Guard
shortDescription: Escort fighter responsible for caravan security, route defense, and protective travel combat.
contextNotes: Escort and route-security martial subtype for caravans and overland trade.

name: Cavalry
shortDescription: Mounted fighting subtype trained in mobility, lance use, and mounted battlefield action.
contextNotes: Proper cavalry should normally know both lance and sidearm use, plus ride, dodge, and parry. This is the broad mounted-war subtype rather than a noble-specific retainer branch.

name: Champion
shortDescription: Elite personal combat specialist known for superior martial skill and reputation.
contextNotes: Fits dueling, elite retainer, household, arena, or heroic combat cultures.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Elite martial subtype.

name: Chariot Driver
shortDescription: Vehicle-and-team specialist skilled in driving animal-drawn war or transport chariots.
contextNotes: Mounted/vehicle subtype for societies where chariot combat or prestige transport is relevant.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Clan Warriors
shortDescription: Kin-based martial subtype trained through collective warfare, raid culture, and clan obligation.
contextNotes: Close to tribal warrior but with stronger social identity as a communal fighting body.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Conman
shortDescription: Fraud specialist using lies, confidence, and social manipulation for gain.
contextNotes: Urban and commercial criminal subtype centered on persuasion and deception rather than entry or force.

name: Courtesan
shortDescription: Elite social specialist using attraction, conversation, and status-sensitive influence.
contextNotes: Belongs in courts, elite cities, and patronage cultures where emotional reading and social access can be professionalized.

name: Crafter
shortDescription: General artisan subtype covering trained making, repair, and workshop practice.
contextNotes: Broad catch-all craft subtype when a more specific building, smithing, or production role is not needed.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Dancer/Acrobat
shortDescription: Dancer/Acrobat source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Docker
shortDescription: Port laborer trained in heavy handling, loading, rope use, and practical waterfront work.
contextNotes: Working subtype for docks, warehouses, quays, and port logistics.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Embalmer
shortDescription: Specialist in body preparation, preservation, and funerary treatment.
contextNotes: Fits temple, urban, or formal mortuary cultures where treatment of the dead is specialized.

name: Entertainer
shortDescription: General public performer combining music, story, movement, and stage presence.
contextNotes: Broad catch-all subtype for traveling, popular, or mixed-form performance.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Entertainers Dancer/Acrobat
shortDescription: Entertainers Dancer/Acrobat source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Entertainers Singer/Musician
shortDescription: Entertainers Singer/Musician source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Entertainers Trickster/Fool
shortDescription: Entertainers Trickster/Fool source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Farmer
shortDescription: Agrarian producer trained in routine husbandry, food production, and practical rural work.
contextNotes: Broad rural livelihood subtype with more emphasis on routine production than mobile pastoral life.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Fisher
shortDescription: Water-working livelihood subtype focused on practical fishing, nets, boats, and local waters.
contextNotes: Lower-threshold maritime subtype for coastal, river, and lake communities.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Fixer
shortDescription: Broker and middleman who arranges goods, favors, access, and informal solutions.
contextNotes: Urban network subtype that blends commerce with social influence and practical problem-solving.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Gladiator
shortDescription: Gladiator source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Arena subtype retained in Soldier.

name: Healer
shortDescription: General healer subtype covering practical treatment, remedies, and routine medical care.
contextNotes: This is a broad catch-all healer for source use when a more specific folk, temple, learned, or court package is not required.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Broad medical catch-all subtype.

name: Heavy Infantry
shortDescription: Disciplined close-combat infantry trained for line fighting, defense, and weight of arms.
contextNotes: Emphasizes sustained melee performance, cohesion, and battlefield durability.

name: Herald
shortDescription: Ceremonial and identity specialist trained in arms, announcements, lineage, and formal representation.
contextNotes: Fits noble, military, and court cultures where heraldic identity, announcements, and formal precedence matter.

name: Herbalist
shortDescription: Plant specialist focused on remedies, materia medica, and useful natural preparations.
contextNotes: Sits between folk healing, pharmacy, and gathered practical knowledge.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Herder
shortDescription: Herder source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Hermit
shortDescription: Solitary spiritual or practical survivalist living apart from ordinary society.
contextNotes: May overlap with holy ascetic, wise recluse, or practical wilderness mystic depending setting.

name: Homemaker
shortDescription: Homemaker source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Hunter
shortDescription: Field worker trained in pursuit, game-taking, terrain use, and practical survival movement.
contextNotes: Rural and wilderness subtype with overlap into light combat or scouting.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Inn Keeper
shortDescription: Inn Keeper source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Jailer
shortDescription: Custodial security specialist responsible for confinement, control, and guarded detention.
contextNotes: Institutional security subtype that fits prisons, castles, barracks, civic jails, or lordly compounds.

name: Lawyer
shortDescription: Legal specialist trained in argument, procedure, and formal disputes.
contextNotes: Belongs in civic, court, bureaucratic, and urban societies where law is a professional practice rather than customary memory alone.

name: Light Infantry
shortDescription: Mobile infantry subtype trained for skirmish, movement, and flexible battlefield action.
contextNotes: Less rigid than levy or heavy infantry; favors movement, missile use, and adaptable positioning.

name: Master Craftsmen
shortDescription: Master Craftsmen source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Merchant
shortDescription: General trade profession managing exchange, value, and practical commercial movement.
contextNotes: This is the broad base merchant subtype, suitable where a more specific trader, factor, or banker package is not needed.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Broad commercial base subtype.

name: Messenger
shortDescription: Messenger source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Military Officer
shortDescription: Martial leader trained in battlefield control, command presence, and tactical judgment.
contextNotes: This subtype emphasizes leadership over pure personal fighting skill. It suits officers, captains, senior sergeants, and unit commanders in organized armed forces.

name: Miner
shortDescription: Extractive labor specialist working underground or in exposed deposits to obtain stone or metal-bearing material.
contextNotes: Heavy practical subtype tied to labor, geology-by-experience, and dangerous extraction work.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Mourner
shortDescription: Ritual or professional funeral participant trained in mourning forms, lament, and death-related observance.
contextNotes: Ceremonial subtype linked to funerary culture, grief ritual, and sometimes religious service.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Musician
shortDescription: Instrumental performance specialist trained in music, rhythm, and public or court presentation.
contextNotes: Covers practical entertainers, ceremonial performers, and elite musicians.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Noble
shortDescription: Elite status-holder trained in rank, ceremony, and practical authority.
contextNotes: Broad noble subtype for courtly, landed, or titled elites. Focuses on etiquette, heraldry, influence, and household-level authority rather than office routine.

name: Outrider/Scout
shortDescription: Outrider/Scout source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Peddler
shortDescription: Small-scale itinerant trader selling goods directly across settlements or roads.
contextNotes: Low-threshold commercial subtype dependent on mobility and practical bargaining rather than major capital.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Personal Servant
shortDescription: Trusted domestic attendant trained in household service, discretion, and elite etiquette.
contextNotes: Household-support subtype for noble, princely, and high-status environments where proximity, manners, and reliability matter.

name: Philosopher
shortDescription: Philosopher source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Pickpocket
shortDescription: Urban theft specialist trained in close-range covert stealing from persons and crowds.
contextNotes: Narrow criminal subtype for dense urban, market, and festival settings.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Politician
shortDescription: Power-seeking public or court actor trained in faction, rhetoric, and practical influence.
contextNotes: Belongs in civic, courtly, and factional settings where persuasion, intrigue, and relationship management are professionalized.

name: Priest
shortDescription: Sacred specialist trained in doctrine, ritual interpretation, and organized religious practice.
contextNotes: This is the formal religious office-holder rather than the broader folk-healer or shamanic type. It belongs especially in temple and literate sacred cultures.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Formal sacred-office subtype.

name: Prospector
shortDescription: Searcher for useful deposits, hidden resources, or economically valuable terrain.
contextNotes: Field subtype sitting between miner, scout, and practical searcher.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Prostitute
shortDescription: Prostitute source-listed occupation subtype.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

name: Prostitute – Courtesan
shortDescription: Sexual-social specialist whose role ranges from survival trade to elite influence depending context.
contextNotes: Handled here as the courtly/high-social end of the source label; lower-end prostitution can be modeled more simply in play.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Sailor
shortDescription: General seafaring worker trained in ordinary shipboard labor, vessel routine, and waterborne travel.
contextNotes: This is the generic sailor subtype beneath more specialized navigators and officers. It fits commercial, military, and fishing vessels.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Generic maritime subtype.

name: Scribe
shortDescription: General literate record-keeper trained in copying, records, and formal writing.
contextNotes: Broad scribal subtype suitable where a more specific temple or court clerk package is not needed.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Shaman
shortDescription: Ritual specialist blending healing, spirit-work, omen reading, and communal guidance.
contextNotes: Best for low-literacy, clan, pastoral, or orally transmitted sacred traditions rather than formal temple bureaucracy.

name: Ships Officer
shortDescription: Command-capable maritime subtype overseeing crew work, route discipline, and shipboard order.
contextNotes: Bridges working seamanship and leadership rather than full independent navigation alone.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Slave master
shortDescription: Harsh supervisory specialist responsible for coerced labor, discipline, and control of dependents.
contextNotes: Authority-and-control subtype for coercive labor systems; placed here because the role is primarily managerial and social-authoritative rather than covert.
notes: Completed package in review pass. Family corrected in civic-control direction.

name: Smuggler
shortDescription: Illicit trader moving goods across borders, tolls, law, or social restrictions.
contextNotes: Commercial-criminal subtype balancing trade skill with concealment and route knowledge.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Illicit trade subtype.

name: Soothsayer
shortDescription: Divinatory specialist focused on signs, omens, and prophetic interpretation.
contextNotes: Sacred or mystical subtype centered more on foretelling and interpretation than on institutional priestly office.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

name: Spy
shortDescription: Covert political operator using disguise, stealth, and controlled social deception.
contextNotes: This subtype belongs in court, state, military, and intrigue-heavy settings where infiltration is at least as social as it is physical.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Covert-political subtype.

name: Street Thug
shortDescription: Urban intimidator or enforcer using threat, violence, and local underworld presence.
contextNotes: Criminal muscle subtype for city and market environments.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Urban enforcer subtype.

name: Student
shortDescription: Formal learner in a literate, scholarly, or institutional training environment.
contextNotes: Broad educational subtype representing someone in active study rather than a fully established profession.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Educational subtype.

name: Tax Collector
shortDescription: Revenue official trained to assess, record, and extract dues, rents, or taxes.
contextNotes: Administrative subtype for literate states, temples, estates, and civic authorities where revenue collection is formalized.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Revenue-office subtype.

name: Thief
shortDescription: General covert criminal operator trained in stealth, entry, concealment, and theft.
contextNotes: This is the broad rogue subtype, suitable where a more specific burglar, pickpocket, or spy package is not needed.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Broad rogue subtype.

name: Torturer
shortDescription: Coercive interrogator or punitive specialist using intimidation, pain, and confinement knowledge.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation. Reassigned from Merchant/Trader to Thief/Infiltrator because the role fits coercive interrogation, prison, and intimidation functions rather than commerce.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Coercive interrogation subtype.

name: Watchman
shortDescription: Low-grade civic guard or patrolman responsible for local watch, alarm, and street order.
contextNotes: Source-listed occupation. Reassigned from Thief/Infiltrator to Soldier because the role is primarily guard, patrol, and low-grade civic security rather than covert intrusion.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Civic guard subtype.

name: Woodcutter
shortDescription: Practical rural labor subtype specializing in timber cutting, woodland work, and heavy manual extraction.
contextNotes: Best treated as a rough craft/labor profession rather than a full engineer or builder in the learned sense.
notes: Completed package in final completion pass.
